title: equisetum palustre f. arcuatum
intro: Equisetum palustre f. arcuatum, also known as Marsh Horsetail, is a subspecies of the Equisetum palustre plant, a type of vascular plant that belongs to the horsetail family (Equisetaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Equisetum palustre f. arcuatum include its ability to help with kidney problems, urinary tract infections, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, although its use should be done under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
